title: O'Reilly Automotive, Inc. (ORLY) Intrinsic Value
description: An independent discounted cash flow (DCF) intrinsic value analysis of O'Reilly Automotive, Inc. (ORLY).

fcfGrowth.title: FCF Growth Rate (Y1-Y5)
fcfGrowth.rationale: A 10.0% growth rate is modeled based on historical execution, addressable market expansion, and ongoing margin optimization efforts.

discountRate.title: Discount Rate (WACC)
discountRate.rationale: A discount rate of 8.0% reflects the company's cost of capital, risk profile, and broader macroeconomic interest rate environment.

terminalGrowth.title: Terminal Growth Rate
terminalGrowth.rationale: A 3.0% terminal rate assumes long-term growth aligning with normalized global GDP expansion and inflation expectations.

thesisParagraphs: O'Reilly Automotive operates one of the most resilient business models in the retail sector. Their success is predicated on the non-discretionary nature of auto repair; when a vehicle breaks down, the consumer or mechanic needs the part immediately, prioritizing availability over marginal price savings. O'Reilly's massive investments in a hub-and-spoke distribution network ensure they can deliver obscure parts to commercial garages faster than competitors, securing deep loyalty in the lucrative B2B segment.

While the distant specter of low-maintenance electric vehicles looms over the industry, the current reality is a U.S. car fleet that is older than ever and strictly reliant on internal combustion engines. O'Reilly functions as an aggressive cannibal, using its massive free cash flow to buy back its own stock while methodically taking market share from regional mom-and-pop stores. This combination of defensive revenue and aggressive financial engineering makes it a formidable long-term compounder.
verdictFooter: Based on latest available financial data. Analysis generated by Gemini.

faq.question: Why was a 10.0% growth rate selected for ORLY?
faq.answer: A 10.0% FCF growth rate is justified by ORLY's combination of steady mid-single-digit top-line growth and their aggressive, continuous share repurchase program. The buybacks mathematically accelerate free cash flow growth strictly on a per-share basis.

faq.question: What discount rate was used for ORLY's DCF?
faq.answer: An 8.0% discount rate was selected. This relatively low rate reflects the highly defensive, non-cyclical nature of the auto repair business and O'Reilly's incredibly predictable historical cash flows.

faq.question: Does this analysis account for the rise of Electric Vehicles?
faq.answer: The terminal growth rate is capped at 3.0% to conservatively account for the long-term risk posed by EVs, which require fewer replacement parts. However, the first 10 years of the model assume ICE vehicles will dominate the repair market based on the current age of the fleet.
